analysis: Fleet Beta Squad 12 — Proactive Enhancement Opportunities

opportunities.observation: 16 MB, 262k lines, never rotated. Contains 12469 ERROR entries. Growing continuously with file watcher and server lifecycle events.
opportunities.recommendation: Add to resource-watchdog log rotation at 5 MB threshold. The .log.1 files exist for gateway but not for agent-memory-server.

opportunities.observation: 22 MB, 301k lines, never rotated. 12.5% of content is the same Errno 48 error repeated 37672 times.
opportunities.recommendation: Rotate at 5 MB. Also fix the root cause: add SO_REUSEADDR to the TCPServer or use a process manager that waits for port release before restart.

opportunities.observation: gateway.log.1 is 52 MB — rotated but the rotated file itself was never cleaned up. Active gateway.log is only 325 KB.
opportunities.recommendation: Delete or compress .log.1 files after rotation. Add gzip compression to rotation script.

opportunities.observation: gateway.err.log.1 is 21 MB, sitting alongside the active 784 KB file. Old rotated log never cleaned.
opportunities.recommendation: Same as gateway.log.1 — compress or delete after rotation.

opportunities.observation: 5 Twitter-related cron jobs all disabled: Twitter Auto-Post Morning/Midday/Evening, Twitter RE Engagement, Twitter Viral Trend Analysis. Twitter DSCR Content Automation and LinkedIn DSCR Content also disabled. Job configs still in jobs.json consuming memory and cluttering job list.
opportunities.recommendation: Archive disabled Twitter job configs to jobs_archive.json and remove from active jobs.json. Delete or archive twitter_bot workspace data.

opportunities.observation: 15 disabled jobs in jobs.json: Polymarket Opportunity Scanner, Hourly Contact Enrichment, Dashboard Data Refresh, 5 Twitter jobs, Instagram Reel, whale-tracker, SMS Health Check, Enhanced Autoresearch, EVOLVE MODE, dashboard-keepalive, Escalation Alerter, Memory Server Health Monitor, Skill Optimizer, LinkedIn engagement. These add parsing overhead and confusion.
opportunities.recommendation: Move all disabled jobs to an archive file. Only keep active jobs in jobs.json.

opportunities.observation: 48 hourly glint snapshot files accumulating in /data/ directory. Each run creates a new timestamped file plus overwrites glint_latest.json. Old snapshots are never cleaned.
opportunities.recommendation: Add daily cleanup: keep only last 24 hourly snapshots and archive older ones. Or rotate to a single rolling file.

opportunities.observation: 48 hourly Kalshi scan files accumulating identically to glint. Same pattern: timestamped files plus a kalshi_scan.json latest pointer.
opportunities.recommendation: Same cleanup strategy as glint — keep 24h window, archive or delete older scans.

opportunities.observation: 18 daily DSCR lead files with duplicate timestamps (e.g., two files per day: _190155 and _190302). Suggests pipeline running twice per day unnecessarily.
opportunities.recommendation: Deduplicate runs — check why pipeline fires twice. Clean up old daily files after upload to ActiveCampaign.

opportunities.observation: Seller financing agent consistently scores 816 leads but qualifies ZERO (HOT=0, WARM=0). This has been the case for every run — the job runs successfully but produces no actionable output. 4 days of runs in logs, all with identical zero output.
opportunities.recommendation: Review scoring thresholds in seller_distress.py. Either lower the qualifying bar (min_score=60 may be too high) or enrich lead data with additional distress signals to improve scoring.

opportunities.observation: Runs every 30 minutes with a 49% failure rate. When it succeeds, it reports 'HEALTHY (0 Actions Taken)' but still consumes full agent session resources. Most runs are no-ops that burn LLM tokens.
opportunities.recommendation: Add a lightweight pre-check before spawning full agent session. If no alerts/actions needed, skip the expensive LLM call. Or reduce frequency to hourly.

opportunities.observation: Every cron job requests claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 which is 'not allowed', causing 746 fallback warnings in gateway.err.log. Each job start triggers this warning — pure noise that obscures real errors.
opportunities.recommendation: Update all job payload.model fields from 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001' to the allowed model in agents.defaults.model.primary, or add haiku to allowed models list.

opportunities.observation: Two separate DSCR lead generation jobs run at the same time (both at 15:00): 'Daily DSCR Lead Generation' (6dd0958b) and 'DSCR Lead Pipeline (Daily)' (6b250f66). They use different scripts (batchdata_pipeline.py vs dscr_lead_scraper.py) but target the same outcome.
opportunities.recommendation: Consolidate into one job or stagger them. Running both at 3PM doubles API costs and creates race conditions in ActiveCampaign uploads.

opportunities.observation: API keys stored in plaintext in openclaw.json env block: OPENROUTER_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY. Also BatchData and ActiveCampaign keys hardcoded in cron job message payloads.
opportunities.recommendation: Move API keys to environment variables or a secrets manager. Remove from cron job payload text where they are visible in logs and run history.

opportunities.observation: 6 backup files of openclaw.json (bak through bak.5) plus openclaw.json.lock and openclaw.json.save. 73 MB of gateway log backups. Total unnecessary disk usage from stale backups.
opportunities.recommendation: Keep only 2 most recent backups. Add automatic cleanup for .bak files older than 7 days.

opportunities.observation: No dedicated health check endpoint. Watchdog checks mission-control port 3000 but does not verify agent-memory-server on port 8100. Server could be down without detection.
opportunities.recommendation: Add /health endpoint to agent-memory-server FastAPI app. Add port 8100 check to resource-watchdog.

opportunities.observation: Task executor has no health monitoring. It rate-limits itself for hours ('Rate limit active, skipping poll' repeated for 90+ minutes) with no external visibility into whether it is healthy or stuck.
opportunities.recommendation: Add health status endpoint or heartbeat file that watchdog can check. Expose rate limit state so operators know when executor is throttled vs dead.

opportunities.observation: Chrome renderer process consuming 1 GB+ RAM for openclaw browser automation. Resource watchdog shows it as top memory consumer. Browser user-data dir is at /Users/marcmunoz/.openclaw/browser/openclaw/user-data with remote debugging on port 18800.
opportunities.recommendation: Evaluate if browser automation is still needed. If only used occasionally, launch on-demand rather than keeping persistent. Could reclaim 1 GB RAM.

opportunities.observation: Polymarket scanner is disabled but its workspace still contains a full Python venv, trading env, and analysis data. The bot.py, whale_tracker.py, and trading scripts are unused.
opportunities.recommendation: Since Polymarket Opportunity Scanner is disabled, archive the workspace or at minimum delete the venv to reclaim disk space.

opportunities.observation: Job e7b7c843 is enabled and running but has 100% failure rate (59 errors, 0 successes in 7 days). Every run consumes LLM tokens and produces nothing.
opportunities.recommendation: Disable until root cause is fixed. Currently burning API credits every run cycle for zero value.

opportunities.observation: Job 2a210f51 has 100% failure rate (5 errors, 0 successes). Enabled but never succeeds.
opportunities.recommendation: Disable until fixed. Investigate why all runs fail — likely missing dependency or API access issue.

opportunities.observation: No alerting when cron jobs enter consecutive error state. Three jobs currently at consecErrors=2 with no notification sent. Escalation Alerter job exists but is disabled.
opportunities.recommendation: Re-enable Escalation Alerter or build lightweight consecutive-error detection into the gateway. Alert on consecErrors >= 2.

opportunities.observation: DSCR email files exist (dscr_emails_20260401_*.json) but the nexus coordinator reports 'Fix data:dscr_emails: No data' as a stuck task. Data exists but downstream consumers cannot find or parse it.
opportunities.recommendation: Fix file path references in the consumption pipeline. Standardize output location for all DSCR pipeline stages.

opportunities.observation: The main agent workspace directory name includes the full API key: 'workspacesk-ant-api03-q4FS2s4URwiAy8KwhGf0pDx-...'. This leaks the API key in every log entry, process listing, and config audit.
opportunities.recommendation: Rename workspace directory to remove the API key from the path. Update all references in openclaw.json.

quick_wins: Disable pattern-detection-scanner and tax-deed-scanner (save API credits immediately)
Fix model spec in cron jobs from haiku to allowed model (eliminate 746 gateway warnings)
Add log rotation for mission-control.log and agent-memory-server.log
Re-enable Escalation Alerter for consecutive error detection
highest_impact: Consolidate duplicate DSCR lead gen jobs (reduce API costs, eliminate race conditions)
Fix seller-financing scoring thresholds (unlock value from 816 leads currently wasted)
Kill persistent Chrome browser when not in use (reclaim 1 GB RAM)
Rename workspace directory to remove API key from path (security fix)
